name: Dockerfile Linting and Security
description: Lint Dockerfiles for best practice violations and security issues using Hadolint to catch problems before building.

steps.title: Lint a Dockerfile with Hadolint
steps.description: Run Hadolint to check for Dockerfile best practice violations.
steps.tip: Hadolint checks shell commands with ShellCheck and validates Dockerfile instructions against best practices.

steps.title: Lint with specific rules ignored
steps.description: Suppress specific rules that do not apply to your use case.
steps.command: docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint hadolint --ignore DL3008 --ignore DL3009 - < Dockerfile
steps.tip: DL3008 warns about pinning apt package versions. DL3009 warns about deleting apt lists.

steps.title: Check for USER instruction
steps.description: Verify the Dockerfile does not run as root in production.
steps.command: grep -n 'USER' Dockerfile || echo 'WARNING: No USER instruction found. Container will run as root.'
steps.warning: Running containers as root is a security risk. Always add a USER instruction for production images.

steps.title: Verify no secrets in build args
steps.description: Check that no sensitive values are passed as build arguments, which are visible in image history.
steps.command: docker history myapp:latest --no-trunc | grep -i 'ARG\|ENV' | grep -i 'password\|secret\|key\|token' || echo 'No obvious secrets found'

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Dockerfile Linting and Security Check
set -euo pipefail

DOCKERFILE=${1:-"Dockerfile"}

echo "=== Linting $DOCKERFILE ==="
docker run --rm -i hadolint/hadolint < "$DOCKERFILE" || true

echo ""
echo "=== Security Checks ==="
echo "--- USER instruction ---"
grep -n 'USER' "$DOCKERFILE" || echo "WARNING: No USER instruction found"

echo ""
echo "--- HEALTHCHECK instruction ---"
grep -n 'HEALTHCHECK' "$DOCKERFILE" || echo "INFO: No HEALTHCHECK instruction found"

echo ""
echo "--- Exposed ports ---"
grep -n 'EXPOSE' "$DOCKERFILE" || echo "No EXPOSE instruction found"

echo ""
echo "--- COPY vs ADD ---"
grep -n 'ADD' "$DOCKERFILE" && echo "Consider using COPY instead of ADD unless you need tar extraction" || echo "Good: No ADD instructions found"

faq.question: What are the most important Hadolint rules?
faq.answer: DL3000 (use WORKDIR), DL3002 (do not switch to root), DL3007 (pin base image tag), and DL3025 (use JSON form for CMD) are critical for security and reliability.

faq.question: Can I use Hadolint in CI/CD?
faq.answer: Yes. Run the Hadolint Docker container in your CI pipeline or install the binary directly. It returns a non-zero exit code on warnings/errors.
